oxen-planner.oxen_target: minimum long-context target; 262144 is experimental until measured on Ubuntu

notes: The facade may be up while the backend is intentionally stopped.
Start the backend only after checking render-node ownership.
MCP is the primary agent control surface.
Denning is Windows/VidMm data; on AM4 Ubuntu, use it as bounds, not as the control law.
SYCL multi-card failed on Windows 10; first AM4 proof is Linux SYCL multi-card correctness.
Linux memory pinning should simplify placement; still measure P2P/card-to-card throughput and engine placement behavior before designing around KV-on-one-card/model-on-another.
With 32 GB DDR, pooled/shared memory is not the first plan; keep steady-state inside B70 VRAM and treat host memory as scarce.
NATS is optional and only needed for durable run events or queue fanout.
The oxen facade HTTP is authenticated; credentials live only on AM4 under ~/.config/am4-fleet.

A headless Valheim dedicated server ('Comfy Era16 Lab', UDP 2456/2457, valheim_server.x86_64 -nographics -batchmode) is a standing tenant. It uses CPU + host RAM only and holds NO render node (verified 2026-07-09 via fuser) — it never contends for the B70s and never appears in the GPU occupancy probe. See B70-CARD-MANAGEMENT.md > Non-GPU tenants.
